titleStmt.title: Letter from Maria Coyle to Hanna Sheehy Skeffington, 20 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Maria Coyle

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Maria Coyle (b.1843) to Hanna Sheehy Skeffington (1877-1946). Coyle writes to Skeffington in order to return tickets for the fundraising concert in aid of the "Irish Citizen". While unhappy to hear that the paper is struggling Coyle does not believe it will make much difference with the passing of the Home Rule Bill. Coyle argues that women's rights will not have a hope in Ireland as Irishmen will never tolerate freedom of their women. Coyle goes on to congratulate Skeffington on her achievements and while she disagrees with certain methods expresses a continued fondness for those involved in the struggle for suffrage.
Hanna Sheehy Skeffington, suffragette, nationalist, language teacher, was the widow of Francis Sheehy-Skeffington (who was summarily executed on 26 April 1916). She was active during the Rising, bringing food to the Volunteers in the G.P.O. and the College of Surgeons. Four days passed before she found out what had happened to her husband, Francis (1878-1916), and it wasn't until almost two weeks later that the full details of his execution emerged.

msIdentifier.collection: Sheehy Skeffington Papers, Collection List No. 47; MS 33,604/16-25

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
24 Charleston Road 20th Feb 1916 My dear Mrs Skeffington I was glad to receive
your memo, as it gave
me an opportunity
of apologizing for my
neglect in not writing
in reply to former com-
munications, one of which
was an application
for my annual subscription.
No doubt that was sent
by mistake & probably
you then remembered
that in May 1913 I had
resigned my membership.
& had not since exercised
any privilege in con
nection with it. I return the tickets which
only reached this morning
in any case would not
have gone myself and
the time was too short
to find any one to take
there. I am sorry to hear
of the non-success of the
paper, but I really feel
that it does not now
much matter, as since
the "Home Rule Bill"
has passed, the women's
movement has no hope
in Ireland. Irishmen
will never tolerate the
Freedom of their women.
I will however send on a very
small trifle, it is all
I can afford towards
your deliverance and
indeed you all have
mt sincere sympathy
& good wishes in the
struggle & self denial
you have made
for a worthy object.
I believe that the
splendid conduct
of the women during
this terrible crisis
is entirely due to the
disciplinary educational
struggle which you, and
your sisters have
reiterated and carried
on during these years
passed and although
I do not approve of
some methods adopted,
I will always entertain
a deep regard for my
former friends in our
suffrage movement &
now thank them for
their kindness and
consideration during
the pleasant evenings
we have passed together
& for their toleration
for one who only now
"cumbereth the ground." Very Sincerely
yours M Coyle
